title: Klaus And Greta

jokes.text: the wine turned out to be quite toxic.
jokes.setup: Jack describes an elaborate New Year's story with ancient wine from a sunken ship
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that the fancy ancient wine was poisonous
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of absurd consequence. Classic 30 Rock style of treating bizarre situations matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: my guests and i spent the stroke of midnight in my garden. vomiting.
jokes.setup: Building on the toxic wine revelation
jokes.punchline: The elegant New Year's celebration devolved into group vomiting
jokes.explanation: Escalates the absurdity while maintaining Jack's pretentious tone. The pause before 'vomiting' sells it.

jokes.text: the whole night the purging,the new year, the vivid hallucinations of astarte the phoenician goddess of sex and war it all wiped the slate clean.
jokes.setup: Jack continuing to describe his disastrous New Year's
jokes.punchline: Treating hallucinations and vomiting as spiritually cleansing experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack Donaghy - turning a horrible experience into profound wisdom with obscure mythological references.

jokes.text: randy's gay,everybody! he's gay. i think everyone knew. finally!
jokes.setup: Liz thinks she helped Randy come out
jokes.punchline: Randy's sexuality was obvious to everyone; Liz's announcement is unnecessary
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Liz's self-importance deflated. Classic sitcom misunderstanding.

jokes.text: over the break,i forgot what floor i worked on. six,tracy. six! i knew it was a character from blossom,but i couldn't find the joey russo button.
jokes.setup: Tracy forgot what floor he works on
jokes.punchline: He knows it's a Blossom character but confuses Six with Joey Lawrence's character
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy Jordan logic - remembering obscure 90s TV trivia but forgetting basic work info.

jokes.text: my client,james franco,he's actively looking for a relationship with a human woman to dispel certain unsavory rumors.
jokes.setup: Manager explaining James Franco's dating situation
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific phrase 'human woman' implies something weird
jokes.explanation: The phrase 'human woman' is perfectly placed to suggest something is off without being explicit.

jokes.text: does chewing on a sponge trick your brain's hunger center? yes! yes!a million times yes!
jokes.setup: Asked if she wants to date James Franco
jokes.punchline: Jenna responds with bizarre diet tip instead of direct answer
jokes.explanation: Peak Jenna - completely random diet advice delivered with romantic enthusiasm. Perfectly in character.

jokes.text: can you believe they went to me before ayiiia from the real world: cancun?
jokes.setup: Jenna excited about being chosen for fake relationship
jokes.punchline: She's surprised they picked her over an obscure reality TV contestant
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jenna's warped perception of her place in Hollywood hierarchy.

jokes.text: too late! wait,which way did you mean that?
jokes.setup: Liz warns Jenna not to let the manager screw her
jokes.punchline: Jenna reveals she already slept with him, then realizes the misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Clean double entendre with good timing on Jenna's realization.

jokes.text: no,no,no,no. okay,but i was gonna describe it real good.
jokes.setup: Kenneth announces his wife might be pregnant
jokes.punchline: Everyone's disgusted reaction stops his planned graphic description
jokes.explanation: Classic Kenneth - inappropriate oversharing with childlike enthusiasm.

jokes.text: as you know,on new year's,i finally met my birth parents. so. thank you for asking about that!
jokes.setup: Jack asks how Kenneth's New Year was
jokes.punchline: Kenneth sarcastically thanks Jack for asking about meeting his birth parents, which Jack clearly forgot
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of Kenneth showing passive-aggressive sass, perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: damn that phoenician wine!
jokes.setup: Jack realizes he called Nancy while drunk
jokes.punchline: Blames the ancient poisonous wine from earlier
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the opening absurdity, maintains Jack's tendency to blame exotic circumstances.

jokes.text: which is the third best sex after elevator and white house.
jokes.setup: Jack mentions Nancy having beach sex
jokes.punchline: His oddly specific ranking of sex locations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment - treating sex like a business ranking with implied elite experience.

jokes.text: good lord,i've lemoned the situation with nancy!
jokes.setup: Jack realizes he's messed up with Nancy
jokes.punchline: Uses 'Lemoned' as a verb meaning to screw something up
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay turning Liz's name into a verb for screwing up. Became a fan favorite quote.

jokes.text: "lemoned"? that's not a thing people are saying now,is it? cerie? lemoned. doing it awesome.
jokes.setup: Liz questions if 'Lemoned' is actually a thing
jokes.punchline: Young Cerie confirms it's already trendy slang
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing how quickly pop culture moves and Liz's disconnect from trends.

jokes.text: this movie will never be released because my performance will be deemed "too provocative for america." oh,i wish i lived in france.
jokes.setup: Franco explains the fake movie backstory
jokes.punchline: His pretentious claim about being too provocative for American audiences
jokes.explanation: Good parody of pretentious actor behavior and American vs. European art sensibilities.

jokes.text: and because of a product placement deal with jamba juice,the fight will be in a jamba juice.
jokes.setup: Franco explaining the fake relationship rules
jokes.punchline: Product placement dictating where they have to fight
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of how product placement corrupts even fake celebrity relationships.

jokes.text: neither am i,liz! which is why it's so weird the tabloids are running all these stories saying i'm in love with a body pillow.
jokes.setup: Franco claims ignorance about body pillow relationships
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals the specific tabloid stories about him
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - his denial immediately contradicted by specific knowledge of the accusations.

jokes.text: why? i mean,why is this table here? i mean,why is that lamp here? kimiko is it is here like any other object.
jokes.setup: Liz points out the suspicious body pillow
jokes.punchline: Franco's desperate philosophical rationalization for why it's there
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Franco's weak attempts to rationalize the obvious.

jokes.text: objects are made by men and used for many purposes. but we never love objects.
jokes.setup: Franco continuing his philosophical defense
jokes.punchline: Stating people don't love objects while clearly in love with an object
jokes.explanation: Strong dramatic irony as Franco denies exactly what he's doing.

jokes.text: nope.that's a serial killer. just get a cab.i'll pay for it. you have amish here?
jokes.setup: Randy mentions someone offering to help him move a couch
jokes.punchline: Liz immediately identifies it as a serial killer, Randy asks about Amish
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Liz's city paranoia and Randy's rural confusion.

jokes.text: i'm going to drive up to waltham,massachusetts,break into a woman's home, and erase a potentially embarrassing and destructive answering machine message. the home in questions has a doggy door. that will be our way in.
jokes.setup: Jack needs help with a 'special assignment'
jokes.punchline: Casually describes an elaborate breaking and entering plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack - treating felony breaking and entering as a routine business task.

jokes.text: prove that you are lithe enough to accompany me to waltham.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining he needs help breaking in
jokes.punchline: Making Kenneth audition for criminal activity with agility test
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment treating crime like a job interview with physical requirements.

jokes.text: you sicken me.
jokes.setup: Jonathan fails the agility test
jokes.punchline: Jack's disgusted dismissal
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction beat. Jack's contempt for physical weakness.

jokes.text: i know it's a girl,liz lemon,because i yelled "susan b.anthony" at the moment of conception.
jokes.setup: Tracy hopes for a daughter
jokes.punchline: His belief that shouting a suffragette's name during sex determines gender
jokes.explanation: Peak Tracy Jordan absurdity. Combining sex ed ignorance with feminist history in the most inappropriate way possible.

jokes.text: so we gonna name her either virginia,netjet,or bathroom at teterboro airport.
jokes.setup: Tracy says they'll name the baby after where she was conceived
jokes.punchline: The increasingly inappropriate location options
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from normal to private jet to airport bathroom. Shows Tracy's rich lifestyle and poor judgment.

jokes.text: the gossip blogs are calling us "james." it's a combination of jenna and james.
jokes.setup: Jenna excited about media coverage of fake relationship
jokes.punchline: The celebrity couple name is just 'James' - basically erasing Jenna
jokes.explanation: Brilliant commentary on celebrity couple names and how they often favor the male celebrity. Perfect Jenna moment.

jokes.text: having ice cold diarrhea from drinking too much jamba juice it's everything i ever wanted.
jokes.setup: Jenna describing her happiness with fame
jokes.punchline: Including the gross side effect as part of her dream life
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the Jamba Juice placement, shows Jenna's willingness to suffer for fame.

jokes.text: that happens sometimes when i unhinge my pelvis.
jokes.setup: Kenneth making a weird noise or movement
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of unhinging his pelvis like a snake
jokes.explanation: Classic Kenneth weirdness - casual mention of impossible anatomy like it's normal.

jokes.text: it'll be like the da vinci code. ehh albino monk! that's a mirror,kenneth.
jokes.setup: Jack compares their mission to The Da Vinci Code
jokes.punchline: Kenneth immediately gets scared of his own reflection thinking it's the albino monk
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy inference - Kenneth's religious paranoia and lack of self-awareness.

jokes.text: i just took my picture with that little camera thing. kenneth! all right. i just need to erase that picture. whoops,i just made it the desktop image.
jokes.setup: Kenneth accidentally takes a photo on the computer
jokes.punchline: Tries to fix it but makes it worse by setting it as wallpaper
jokes.explanation: Standard tech-incompetent character making things worse while trying to fix them.

jokes.text: that's where i'm meeting them later - a bar called "home butt." no,you're not.
jokes.setup: Randy mentions meeting the guys who gave him a ride
jokes.punchline: The bar name 'Home Butt' is obviously a gay bar that Randy doesn't recognize
jokes.explanation: Good fish-out-of-water humor showing Randy's innocence about gay bar culture.

jokes.text: the local tv station edited will & grace down so much that it was just called karen.
jokes.setup: Randy explaining how hard it was being gay in his hometown
jokes.punchline: The local station censored Will & Grace so heavily only Karen's scenes remained
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of homophobic censorship and small-town attitudes. Economical and perfectly crafted.

jokes.text: your hand feels like a pillow that's been in the microwave.
jokes.setup: Jenna and James holding hands for fake romance
jokes.punchline: Her oddly specific and unromantic description of his hand
jokes.explanation: Perfectly weird Jenna simile - specific, unromantic, and strangely vivid.

jokes.text: i thought it was a paparazzo,but it's just some loser taking a picture of his kid.
jokes.setup: Jenna reacting to someone taking photos
jokes.punchline: Dismisses a father photographing his child as a 'loser' because it's not about her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna self-absorption - can't comprehend why anyone would take photos not involving her.

jokes.text: kimiko taught me that.
jokes.setup: Franco shares trivia about paparazzi terminology
jokes.punchline: Credits his body pillow girlfriend with teaching him
jokes.explanation: Subtle callback showing Franco's deepening delusion about his relationship with the pillow.

jokes.text: if you type "nancy's secrets" into the internet,do you have any idea what comes up? a store that sells wig extensions.
jokes.setup: Kenneth asking about searching for 'Nancy's secrets' online
jokes.punchline: Instead of something scandalous, it's just a mundane beauty supply store
jokes.explanation: Good anti-climax joke and Kenneth's innocent approach to internet searching.

jokes.text: he was eventually arrested by israeli commandos.
jokes.setup: Jack mentioning their high school German teacher Mr. Kruger
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that he was a Nazi war criminal
jokes.explanation: Perfect throwaway line with dark implications delivered casually. Classic 30 Rock historical horror as comedy.

jokes.text: did you not learn your nation's airport codes in high school?
jokes.setup: Kenneth doesn't know airport codes
jokes.punchline: Jack's assumption that airport codes are basic education
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack elitism - treating obscure knowledge as universal education failure.

jokes.text: that's the most divorced sentence i've ever heard.
jokes.setup: Jack says 'She's at the beach house alone!'
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's innocent but perceptive observation about divorce clichés
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observation about divorce tropes. Kenneth's innocence leads to sharp social insight.

jokes.text: oh,my god,did you go out last night after i won the sleeping contest?
jokes.setup: Liz seeing Randy with makeup/glitter
jokes.punchline: Her delusion that their early bedtime was a 'contest' she won
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz moment - reframing her boring night as a victory to maintain superiority.

jokes.text: is it gonna be fierce? it would be if it was 2006.
jokes.setup: Randy offering to give Liz a makeover
jokes.punchline: Randy's acknowledgment that 'fierce' is outdated slang
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about how quickly gay slang becomes outdated and mainstream.

jokes.text: okay,i do appreciate the irony that i'm the one stuck in the closet now. is that irony? not really.
jokes.setup: Liz locked in a closet while Randy (recently out) is outside
jokes.punchline: Her attempt at irony followed by immediate self-correction
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about misusing literary terms, classic Liz overthinking.

jokes.text: sat behind you so i could talk to you and try to see the top of your underwear.
jokes.setup: Jack's drunk romantic voicemail to Nancy
jokes.punchline: His teenage motivation was both sweet and creepy
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy balancing romantic nostalgia with teenage creepiness.

jokes.text: ballard,don't let them put their tops back on.
jokes.setup: Jack's drunk voicemail continues
jokes.punchline: Background audio reveals the debauchery happening during the call
jokes.explanation: Great layered joke - romantic voicemail undercut by hedonistic party in background.

jokes.text: but you are leaving through the doggy door.
jokes.setup: Jack decides to let Kenneth leave after moral lecture
jokes.punchline: Still makes Kenneth use the humiliating exit as punishment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack pettiness - accepting the moral lesson but still asserting dominance.

jokes.text: i like to take the shampoo and put it in the little travel size bottles. then i take the original bottle and put it back in the shower. for later.
jokes.setup: Tracy playing the Today Show drinking game
jokes.punchline: His unnecessary and backwards approach to travel-sized toiletries
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy moment showing his wealth-warped logic and overthinking simple tasks.

jokes.text: if you want to save money on the trip,consider taking a sandwich.
jokes.setup: Today Show giving travel advice to gay couple
jokes.punchline: Insultingly basic money-saving tip for a wedding trip
jokes.explanation: Decent satire of condescending TV travel advice and heteronormative assumptions.

jokes.text: and before you worked here,were you an ass scientist? because your ass blah,blah,blah.you get the point.
jokes.setup: Tracy hitting on a new woman
jokes.punchline: He starts a crude pickup line but gives up mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about crude pickup lines and Tracy's lazy approach to flirtation.

jokes.text: virginia? but that's gonna be my daughter's name. are you also someone's daughter? uh,yeah. is every woman someone's daughter?
jokes.setup: Tracy learns the woman's name is Virginia
jokes.punchline: His dawning realization about the basic fact that all women are daughters
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Tracy moment - his mind blown by basic family relationships. Perfect escalating confusion.

jokes.text: they spell "klaus." your name in german class.
jokes.setup: Kenneth figuring out Nancy's voicemail code
jokes.punchline: The numbers spell Jack's German class name - she's been thinking of him
jokes.explanation: Good romantic revelation with numerical wordplay. Pays off the German class setup.

jokes.text: you should buy a leather jacket!
jokes.setup: Jack needs to figure out his next move with Nancy
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's hilariously inappropriate advice for romantic strategy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth - his pure heart leads to completely wrong romantic advice.

jokes.text: stop so bored. did i do that right? yeah. that was super bitchy.
jokes.setup: Randy teaching Liz to be mean
jokes.punchline: His validation of her weak attempt at attitude
jokes.explanation: Decent role-reversal with Randy coaching Liz on attitude, though relies on stereotypes.

jokes.text: unless kimiko's jealous of the ottoman. there's nothing going on there.that's a business relationship.
jokes.setup: Franco worried about making Kimiko jealous
jokes.punchline: Clarifying his relationship with furniture is strictly professional
jokes.explanation: Peak absurdist humor - Franco treating furniture relationships with human relationship logic.

jokes.text: don't lemon your life,james.
jokes.setup: Jenna giving James advice about happiness
jokes.punchline: Using 'lemon' as a verb for screwing up your life
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the 'lemoned' verb with Jenna surprisingly giving good advice.

jokes.text: and i'm in love with and common law married to a japanese body pillow!
jokes.setup: Franco declaring he'll be true to himself
jokes.punchline: Proudly announcing his relationship with the pillow
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of the body pillow storyline with Franco's passionate declaration.

jokes.text: randy,this is james franco and our friend kimiko tan.
jokes.setup: Liz introducing Randy to her guests
jokes.punchline: Casually introducing a body pillow as if it's a person
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag with Liz's matter-of-fact acceptance of the bizarre situation.

jokes.text: you have created an atmosphere of hostility and intolerance that everyone talks about all the time.
jokes.setup: Tracy blaming his entourage for bad attitude toward women
jokes.punchline: Claiming their sexism is universally discussed
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy projection - blaming others for his own behavior

jokes.text: since the phone call i interrupted to make this announcement?
jokes.setup: Tracy asks when Grizz last told his fiancée he loved her
jokes.punchline: Grizz's matter-of-fact response about the timing
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response showing Grizz's healthy relationship contrasting with Tracy's issues.

jokes.text: kenneth,your haircut is disrespectful to lesbians!
jokes.setup: Tracy on a rant about attitudes toward women
jokes.punchline: Random accusation about Kenneth's haircut
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy non-sequitur. Absurd accusation that somehow makes sense in his worldview.

jokes.text: but george eliot was a woman.
jokes.setup: Tracy asks if Dotcom reads books by women
jokes.punchline: Dotcom's factual but missing-the-point response
jokes.explanation: Good intellectual humor with Dotcom technically correct but missing Tracy's point about supporting female authors.

jokes.text: to be comtinued..... really?
jokes.setup: Tracy declares he's adding a woman to his entourage
jokes.punchline: Grizz questions the 'to be continued' format
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about TV tropes with characters questioning the show's own structure.

tmdb_overview: Following a wild New Year's Eve, Jack has to try to repair the damage he did after leaving a drunken message on an ex's voicemail. Meanwhile, Jenna tries to get into a relationship with James Franco in order to cover up his secret; and Liz, after accidentally "outing" her cousin to his family, welcomes him to New York City; and Tracy finds new respect for women.
insight: Absurdist escalation reaches 90+ with 2.94 jokes-per-minute—30 Rock's densest sustained assault.
